Organization.title: Organization
Organization.description: Represents a white-label client organization utilizing the SkillSprint platform, serving as the root for multi-tenancy.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Organization entity.

name.description: The official name of the organization.

description.description: A brief description of the organization.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the organization record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the organization record was last updated.

Learner.title: Learner
Learner.description: Represents an individual learner registered under an Organization.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Learner entity.

organizationId.description: Reference to the Organization this learner belongs to. (Relationship: Organization 1:N Learner)

email.description: The learner's email address, used for communication and login (handled by external auth).

firstName.description: The first name of the learner.

lastName.description: The last name of the learner.

enrollmentDate.description: The date when the learner was enrolled.

lastLogin.description: The timestamp of the learner's last login.

currentXp.description: The learner's current total experience points (XP) across all completed modules/quizzes.

currentDailyStreak.description: The learner's current consecutive daily login streak.

lastStreakUpdateDate.description: The date when the learner's daily streak was last updated or maintained.

profileImageUrl.description: Optional URL to the learner's profile image.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the learner's profile was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the learner's profile was last updated.

Course.title: Course
Course.description: Represents a collection of learning modules provided by an Organization.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Course entity.

organizationId.description: Reference to the Organization that owns this course. (Relationship: Organization 1:N Course)

description.description: A detailed description of what the course covers.

difficultyLevel.description: The perceived difficulty level of the course (e.g., 'Beginner', 'Intermediate', 'Advanced').

estimatedCompletionTime.description: An estimate of the time required to complete the course (e.g., '2 hours', '1 week').

tags.description: A list of tags or keywords associated with the course for categorization and search.

isPublished.description: Indicates whether the course is publicly available to learners.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the course was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the course was last updated.

Module.title: Module
Module.description: Represents a bite-sized learning unit within a Course.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Module entity.

courseId.description: Reference to the Course this module belongs to. (Relationship: Course 1:N Module)

description.description: A brief description of the module's content.

contentType.description: The type of content in the module (e.g., 'text', 'video', 'quiz').

contentBody.description: The actual text content for 'text' type modules.

contentLink.description: A URI for external content like video links or hosted documents.

order.description: The sequential order of the module within its parent course.

xpAward.description: The amount of XP a learner gains upon successful completion of this module.

isPublished.description: Indicates whether the module is published and visible to learners.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the module was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the module was last updated.

QuizQuestion.title: QuizQuestion
QuizQuestion.description: Represents a multiple-choice question within a Module of type 'quiz'.

id.description: Unique identifier for the QuizQuestion entity.

moduleId.description: Reference to the Module this question belongs to. (Relationship: Module 1:N QuizQuestion)

questionText.description: The text of the quiz question.

options.description: An array of possible answers for the multiple-choice question, each with text and correctness.

correctAnswerIndexes.description: An array of zero-based indexes indicating which options are correct. Supports single or multiple correct answers.

explanation.description: An optional explanation provided after the learner answers the question.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the quiz question was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the quiz question was last updated.

ProgressLog.title: ProgressLog
ProgressLog.description: Records a specific event of a learner's progress through a module or quiz.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ProgressLog entity.

learnerId.description: Reference to the Learner who performed the action. (Relationship: Learner 1:N ProgressLog)

moduleId.description: Reference to the Module involved in this progress log entry. (Relationship: Module 1:N ProgressLog)

courseId.description: Reference to the Course the module belongs to, for easier querying. (Relationship: Course 1:N ProgressLog)

completionDate.description: Timestamp when the module or quiz was completed/attempted.

xpGained.description: The amount of XP gained for this specific progress event.

quizAttempted.description: Indicates if this log entry represents a quiz attempt.

quizPassed.description: Indicates if the quiz attempt was successful (if quizAttempted is true).

quizScore.description: The score obtained on the quiz (if quizAttempted is true, 0-100).

status.description: The status of the module/quiz progress (e.g., 'started', 'completed', 'passed_quiz', 'failed_quiz').

createdAt.description: Timestamp when this progress log entry was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when this progress log entry was last updated.

AIConversation.title: AIConversation
AIConversation.description: Represents a single conversational session between a Learner and the intelligent AI proctor.

id.description: Unique identifier for the AIConversation entity.

learnerId.description: Reference to the Learner participating in the conversation. (Relationship: Learner 1:N AIConversation)

organizationId.description: Reference to the Organization the learner belongs to, for multi-tenancy. (Relationship: Organization 1:N AIConversation)

startTime.description: Timestamp marking the beginning of the conversation session.

endTime.description: Optional timestamp marking the end of the conversation session.

topicSummary.description: An AI-generated summary of the main topics discussed in the conversation.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the conversation record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the conversation record was last updated.

AIChatMessage.title: AIChatMessage
AIChatMessage.description: Represents an individual message exchanged within an AIConversation.

id.description: Unique identifier for the AIChatMessage entity.

conversationId.description: Reference to the AIConversation this message belongs to. (Relationship: AIConversation 1:N AIChatMessage)

sender.description: Indicates who sent the message ('learner' or 'ai').

timestamp.description: Timestamp when the message was sent.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the chat message record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the chat message record was last updated.

AISuggestion.title: AISuggestion
AISuggestion.description: Represents a course or module suggestion made by the AI proctor to a Learner during a conversation.

id.description: Unique identifier for the AISuggestion entity.

conversationId.description: Reference to the AIConversation during which the suggestion was made. (Relationship: AIConversation 1:N AISuggestion)

learnerId.description: Reference to the Learner for whom the suggestion was made. (Relationship: Learner 1:N AISuggestion)

suggestedEntityType.description: The type of entity being suggested ('Course' or 'Module').

suggestedEntityId.description: Reference to the suggested Course or Module. (Relationship: Course 1:N AISuggestion OR Module 1:N AISuggestion)

reasoning.description: The AI's reasoning or explanation for making this suggestion.

suggestionTimestamp.description: Timestamp when the suggestion was made by the AI.

wasAccepted.description: Optional: Indicates if the learner accepted the suggestion.

acceptedTimestamp.description: Optional: Timestamp when the learner accepted the suggestion.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the AI suggestion record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the AI suggestion record was last updated.

definition.description: Top-level collection for all white-label client organizations. Each document represents a unique organization in the multi-tenant architecture.

params.name: organizationId
params.description: The unique identifier for an Organization.

definition.description: Collection for storing administrators of a specific organization. The existence of a document signifies that the `adminId` (which is the user's UID) is an administrator for this organization, enabling Database Access Control (DBAC). This allows for managing courses, modules, and viewing compliance reports. (Note: Using Learner schema as a placeholder for identity, but actual document content might be minimal, e.g., just the ID itself).

params.name: organizationId
params.description: The unique identifier for the parent Organization.

params.name: adminId
params.description: The Firebase Authentication UID of the administrator.

definition.description: Collection for individual learners registered under a specific organization. This path provides path-based ownership for learner-specific data.

params.name: organizationId
params.description: The unique identifier for the parent Organization.

params.name: learnerId
params.description: The unique identifier for the Learner (corresponds to Firebase Auth UID).

definition.description: Collection for courses offered by a specific organization. Access can be restricted by `isPublished` status, supporting QAPs for listing available courses.

params.name: organizationId
params.description: The unique identifier for the parent Organization.

params.name: courseId
params.description: The unique identifier for the Course.

definition.description: Subcollection for modules belonging to a specific course. Includes denormalized 'organizationId' for authorization independence, allowing rules to check organization membership without `get()` calls on parent documents.

params.name: organizationId
params.description: The unique identifier for the parent Organization.

params.name: courseId
params.description: The unique identifier for the parent Course.

params.name: moduleId
params.description: The unique identifier for the Module.

definition.description: Subcollection for quiz questions within a specific module. Includes denormalized 'organizationId' and 'courseId' for authorization independence, preventing `get()` calls to parent modules or courses for access checks.

params.name: organizationId
params.description: The unique identifier for the parent Organization.

params.name: courseId
params.description: The unique identifier for the parent Course.

params.name: moduleId
params.description: The unique identifier for the parent Module.

params.name: questionId
params.description: The unique identifier for the QuizQuestion.

definition.description: Subcollection for tracking a learner's progress through modules and quizzes. Stored under the learner for direct user ownership and efficient, secure QAPs. Includes denormalized 'organizationId' for admin reporting via collection group queries without parent `get()` calls.

params.name: organizationId
params.description: The unique identifier for the parent Organization.

params.name: learnerId
params.description: The unique identifier for the parent Learner.

params.name: progressLogId
params.description: The unique identifier for the ProgressLog entry.

definition.description: Subcollection for storing AI conversational sessions for a specific learner. Provides path-based ownership for learner's private conversations.

params.name: organizationId
params.description: The unique identifier for the parent Organization.

params.name: learnerId
params.description: The unique identifier for the parent Learner.

params.name: conversationId
params.description: The unique identifier for the AIConversation.

definition.description: Subcollection for individual chat messages within an AI conversation. Includes denormalized 'organizationId' and 'learnerId' for authorization independence, enabling direct access checks on each message.

params.name: organizationId
params.description: The unique identifier for the top-level Organization.

params.name: learnerId
params.description: The unique identifier for the parent Learner.

params.name: conversationId
params.description: The unique identifier for the parent AIConversation.

params.name: messageId
params.description: The unique identifier for the AIChatMessage.

definition.description: Subcollection for AI-generated course/module suggestions made during a conversation. Includes denormalized 'organizationId' for authorization independence.

params.name: organizationId
params.description: The unique identifier for the top-level Organization.

params.name: learnerId
params.description: The unique identifier for the parent Learner.

params.name: conversationId
params.description: The unique identifier for the parent AIConversation.

params.name: suggestionId
params.description: The unique identifier for the AISuggestion.

firestore.reasoning:
The recommended Firestore structure is designed following a multi-tenant hierarchy with an emphasis on Authorization Independence, Structural Segregation, and consistent Access Modeling. The root of the data structure is `/organizations/{organizationId}`, enforcing multi-tenancy as requested. All subsequent collections are subcollections of a specific organization or a learner within an organization, ensuring data isolation and clear ownership.

Authorization Independence (CRITICAL) is achieved through strategic denormalization. For instance, `Module` documents will include `organizationId` in addition to `courseId`, and `QuizQuestion` documents will store `organizationId` and `courseId` alongside `moduleId`. Similarly, `AIChatMessage`, `AISuggestion`, and `ProgressLog` documents will denormalize `organizationId` and `learnerId` (where applicable) directly into their respective documents. This eliminates the need for expensive and rule-breaking `get()` operations on parent documents during authorization checks, allowing for atomic writes and simplified security rules. Rules can directly check `request.auth.uid` against relevant fields like `learnerId` or check for membership in the `/organizations/{organizationId}/admins/{adminId}` collection, ensuring efficient and secure access.

Structural Segregation (Homogeneous Security Posture) is applied by ensuring that all documents within a given collection share similar security requirements. For example, `Course` and `Module` documents utilize an `isPublished` field, and security rules will enforce that only published content is readable by learners. This avoids mixing data with disparate access needs in the same collection, simplifying rule logic. Learner-specific data like `ProgressLog`, `AIConversation`, `AIChatMessage`, and `AISuggestion` are nested directly under `/organizations/{organizationId}/learners/{learnerId}`, creating a clear path-based ownership model.

QAPs (Query as Authorization Predicates) are supported by this structure. For public data within an organization (e.g., published courses, modules), rules will require specific query constraints like `request.query.where('isPublished', '==', true)`. For private, user-owned data (e.g., `progressLogs`, `aiConversations`), rules will ensure that `request.auth.uid == learnerId` for reads, effectively making `list` operations secure by default. Organization administrators, defined by presence in `/organizations/{organizationId}/admins/{adminId}`, can perform collection group queries on learner-specific subcollections (e.g., `progressLogs`) and the denormalized `organizationId` field in each document allows rules to validate their administrative scope without `get()` calls on parent `learner` documents. This holistic approach ensures secure, scalable, and debuggable access control across the platform.
